items.q: How do you say 'I like coffee' (in general) in Spanish?
items.why: English drops the article for general likes; Spanish keeps it. For general preferences, use the DEFINITE article: me gusta EL chocolate, me gustan LOS libros.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: How do you describe weather during a past holiday: 'It was sunny'?
items.why: Weather backdrops in past narratives use IMPERFECT. Preterite implies a single bounded event. 'Cuando llegué, hacía sol' = ongoing weather; llegué = completed action. Standard pattern for holiday writing.

items.q: When was Part I of Don Quijote published?
items.why: It's 1605. “1492” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: era. “fue” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Cervantes die?
items.why: The real figure is 1616. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
